touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Sebastian Brooks introducing our platform's pipeline visibility capabilities. No response received.
LinkedIn message to Sebastian highlighting ROI case study from similar eCommerce company. Sebastian responded positively, expressed interest in learning more about pipeline forecasting accuracy.

questions_asked.question: How does your forecasting compare to what we'd get with Fathom?
questions_asked.answer: Explained our proprietary deal scoring engine and real-time pipeline health monitoring, which provides granular risk assessment beyond basic conversation intelligence.

next_steps: Sebastian agreed to take a brief call next week

touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Sebastian. Left voicemail referencing LinkedIn conversation and suggesting times for qualification call.
Follow-up email with calendar link and brief overview of pipeline visibility features. Sebastian replied confirming Thursday 2pm for qualification call.

13-minute qualification call with Sebastian. He manages a 6-person sales team selling analytics software to eCommerce companies. Currently struggling with forecast accuracy and limited visibility into deal health. Interested in automations and some light consulting to optimize their sales process.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for tracking deal health and forecasting?
questions_asked.answer: Manual updates in spreadsheets. Relies heavily on rep intuition. Forecast accuracy around 60%.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform integrate with our existing CRM without a heavy lift?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with all major CRMs. Typical setup is 1-2 days.

next_steps: Sebastian will loop in Casey Schneider (Sales Manager) for discovery call
Send use case overview and pricing ranges

touches.ai_summary: Sent discovery prep email with agenda and requested Sebastian invite Casey. Included 2-3 customer case studies from eCommerce/SaaS verticals. Sebastian confirmed Casey will join.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled with Sebastian and Casey

touches.ai_summary: 11-minute discovery call with Sebastian and Casey. Discussed current pain points: reps miss follow-ups, no automated risk alerts, Casey spends hours manually reviewing pipeline. Casey is the primary end-user who would manage the platform day-to-day. Team size confirmed at 13 total (6 AEs, 6 SDRs, 1 manager).

questions_asked.question: What specific automations would have the highest impact for your team?
questions_asked.answer: Automated follow-up reminders, deal health alerts, and pipeline reporting for weekly leadership reviews.

questions_asked.question: How much of a time commitment is the consulting piece? We're a lean team.
questions_asked.answer: Light consulting is 2-4 hours per month focused on process optimization and playbook development.

next_steps: Schedule product demo for next week
Sebastian will try to get VP Sales involved for demo

touches.ai_summary: Demo confirmation email sent with personalized agenda based on discovery notes. Included custom ROI estimate showing 15-20 hours/month saved. Sebastian confirmed he and Casey will attend, but VP Sales is unavailable.

questions_asked.question: Is there any concern about moving forward without VP Sales on the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Sebastian says he has budget authority for tools under $15K and will brief VP separately.

next_steps: Sebastian will share recording with VP Sales afterwards

touches.ai_summary: 23-minute demo session with Sebastian and Casey. Walked through pipeline visibility dashboard, automated risk scoring, and follow-up workflows. Both engaged positively. Casey asked detailed questions about customization. Discussed Fathom comparison - acknowledged they're evaluating both but our automation capabilities are differentiated.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the risk scoring criteria to match our sales process?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully customizable. Consulting package includes configuration support.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical discount for companies our size?
questions_asked.answer: Explained standard pricing, mentioned we can work with them on a multi-year commitment for better rates.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Sebastian wants to move quickly - ideally implemented before Q3. Budget is available now.

objections: Casey mentioned Fathom quoted them significantly lower, wants to understand pricing gap.
next_steps: Sebastian will review with finance and VP Sales

touches.ai_summary: Sent detailed proposal: 13 seats, automations + consulting package, $14,060 annual ($1,082/month). Highlighted ROI and included implementation timeline. Sebastian responded asking if there's flexibility on pricing given Fathom's lower quote.

questions_asked.question: Can you come down closer to $10K to match our budget constraints?
questions_asked.answer: Proposed meeting to discuss package options and potential multi-year commitment for better pricing.

next_steps: Negotiation call scheduled to discuss pricing

touches.ai_summary: 28-minute negotiation call with Sebastian and Casey. Discussed budget constraints and Fathom comparison in detail. They're concerned about upfront cost but acknowledged our automation and consulting add clear value Fathom doesn't offer. Explored options: reduced seat count vs. multi-year lock-in.

questions_asked.question: If we could meet your $10-11K target, would you be able to commit today?
questions_asked.answer: Sebastian said yes, pending final VP approval, but he's confident he can get sign-off.

questions_asked.question: What's the minimum commitment term?
questions_asked.answer: Standard is annual, but can discuss quarterly for first year with ramp pricing.

questions_asked.question: Can we reduce consulting hours to lower the price?
questions_asked.answer: Explained consulting is already light at 2-4 hrs/month. Removing it saves ~$1,200 but loses key onboarding support.

objections: Sebastian reiterated Fathom is $3-4K cheaper, needs to justify the premium internally.
next_steps: Rep will prepare revised proposal at $10,600 (24.6% discount)
Sebastian commits to closing this week if pricing works

touches.ai_summary: 13-minute follow-up negotiation call. Presented revised $10,600 offer (13 seats, full package, annual commitment). Sebastian appreciates the flexibility. Casey confirmed they're ready to move forward. Discussed contract terms and implementation kickoff timeline.

questions_asked.question: Are there any other concerns before we finalize?
questions_asked.answer: Sebastian wants to confirm VP Sales is aligned, will brief him today.

next_steps: Sebastian to get VP verbal approval by EOD

touches.ai_summary: 33-minute final negotiation and contract review call with Sebastian and Casey. Walked through MSA and SOW in detail. Sebastian confirmed VP Sales approved the $10,600 pricing. Discussed payment terms (annual upfront), implementation timeline (2-week kickoff), and success metrics. Both parties aligned on moving forward.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Offered quarterly at 5% premium ($11,130 total). Sebastian agreed to annual to keep the $10,600 rate.

questions_asked.question: What does the onboarding process look like?
questions_asked.answer: Detailed 2-week implementation plan: CRM integration, team training, playbook setup, go-live support.

questions_asked.question: Any blockers to signing this week?
questions_asked.answer: None. Sebastian will route contract through legal review (2-3 days), then execute.

next_steps: Implementation kickoff scheduled for following Monday
